1148.reason: [The checkI method is called with the current token before consuming the semicolon. At this point, the current token is the last token of the ConstDef (e.g., a constant value), not the semicolon. The parser should first call getToken() to advance to the next token and then check if it's a semicolon.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The checkI method is called with the current token before consuming the semicolon. At this point, the current token is the last token of the ConstDef (e.g., a constant value), not the semicolon. The parser should first call getToken() to advance to the next token and then check if it's a semicolon.]

1221.reason: [Same issue as in ConstDecl: checkI is called with the current token which is the last token of the VarDef (e.g., an initializer value), not the expected semicolon. The parser needs to advance to the next token first before checking for the semicolon.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Same issue as in ConstDecl: checkI is called with the current token which is the last token of the VarDef (e.g., an initializer value), not the expected semicolon. The parser needs to advance to the next token first before checking for the semicolon.]

1224.reason: [In multiple statement parsing branches (break, continue, return, printf, assignment, etc.), the same pattern occurs: checkI is called with the current token which is the last token of the statement content, not the semicolon that should follow. The parser must call getToken() to advance to the semicolon position before checking.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [In multiple statement parsing branches (break, continue, return, printf, assignment, etc.), the same pattern occurs: checkI is called with the current token which is the last token of the statement content, not the semicolon that should follow. The parser must call getToken() to advance to the semicolon position before checking.]
